_meta.title: Elegoo SDCP (Centauri Carbon 1) — WebSocket frame envelope + topic grammar
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (Elegoo's first-party LAN SDK + the cbd-tech SDCP v3.0 spec); 🔵 the SDCP path is community firmware-validated (Centauri Carbon ~1.1.x)

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Elegoo's own `elegoo-link` SDK (Apache-2.0) + the resin-oriented SDCP v3.0 spec. Struct shapes described, no code copied; no secrets (SDCP has no auth).

notes: SDCP is NOT JSON-RPC 2.0 — there is no `jsonrpc`/`method`/`id`. Correlate on the echoed `RequestID` (a client-generated hex string).
The FDM Centauri reuses the resin SDCP envelope + `Cmd` codes but substitutes FDM field names and repurposes the status enum.
Transport limits: max 5 concurrent WS connections (a 6th connect → HTTP 500); 60s idle timeout. Keep one long-lived socket warm with a periodic `Cmd 0` (no subscribe command exists).

Data.MainboardID: <string — from discovery; also the topic routing key>
Data.From: <int — source enum; a LAN client sends 1>

response_frame.comment: device -> client, on Topic `sdcp/response/<MainboardID>`, echoing the request's RequestID
response_frame.correlation: match on Data.RequestID (echoed); NOT a JSON-RPC id

topics.use: PUSHED machine + PrintInfo state (unsolicited; also the reply to Cmd 0)

From.note: message source; a LAN client sends 1 (as the SDK does)

gotchas: A wrong `MainboardID` makes you publish/subscribe to topics the printer never touches — a SILENT no-op, not an error.
Status arrives UNSOLICITED on sdcp/status — there is no subscribe command; a periodic `Cmd 0` is both poll and keepalive.
The envelope `TimeStamp` is Unix milliseconds — a different unit from the print `CurrentTicks`/`TotalTicks` (seconds) on the same frame.
